name: Zinc Oxide
description: An inorganic mineral compound that provides broad-spectrum UV protection by physically reflecting and scattering ultraviolet radiation, while also serving as a skin protectant, mild antiseptic, and alternative deodorant active ingredient.
appearance: Fine white powder; odorless and tasteless
solubility: Insoluble in water (0.0004 g/100 mL); soluble in dilute acids and alkalis
chemistry: Semiconductor with 3.37 eV band gap. UV protection via absorption (band gap excitation), scattering (Mie), and reflection (high refractive index 2.0). Broad-spectrum UVA+UVB. Zn²⁺ ions are directly antibacterial.
warnings: Do NOT inhale dry powder — can cause metal fume fever. Wear dust mask when handling. Not for ingestion. DIY sunscreen SPF cannot be reliably tested.
shelfLife: Indefinite (stable inorganic mineral); formulations limited by carrier oil shelf life
